metadata.name: lhs_2016_phase3
metadata.description:
Latin Hypercube Sampling sweep targeting the Phase 3 PRIM uncertainty zone
at 2016-block retarget. Samples uniformly within the 50/50 transition region
identified by PRIM boundary fitting (tools/discovery/fit_boundary.py).

PURPOSE: Resolve the decision boundary within the outcome-uncertainty zone.
The Phase 2 PRIM analysis found that 51% of the 2016-block parameter space
(n=152/298) produces outcomes near 50/50. This sweep samples exclusively within
that zone to identify the precise conditions that separate v27 wins from v26 wins.

PRIM UNCERTAINTY BOX (source: tools/discovery/output/2016/uncertainty_bounds.yaml):
  economic_split:        [0.280, 0.779]
  pool_committed_split:  [0.152, 0.526]
  pool_ideology_strength:[0.435, 0.797]
  pool_max_loss_pct:     [0.163, 0.400]
  support: 51.0% of 2016-block data, mean v27_win = 50.0%

DESIGN:
- 300 scenarios via Latin Hypercube Sampling (25 per namespace, 12 namespaces)
- 2 servers × 6 namespaces each (server1: sweep_0000–0149, server2: sweep_0150–0299)
- 4 active parameters only (pool_profitability_threshold and solo_miner_hashrate
  confirmed non-causal at both retarget regimes — fixed at validated values)
- 2016-block retarget (realistic Bitcoin mainnet dynamics)
- Duration 13000s (consistent with lhs_2016_6param for directly comparable results)

EXPECTED RUNTIME (per server, 6 namespaces parallel):
- 150 scenarios per server, 25 per namespace
- ~4 hours wall-clock per scenario (includes pod startup + 13000s simulation)
- ~100 hours sequential per namespace → ~100 hours wall-clock (parallel)

GENERATES: High-resolution boundary map within the 50/50 transition zone.
           Combined with existing 2016-block data (n=298), total n=598 at 2016-block.
           Enables Phase 3 PRIM re-fit and logistic regression boundary refinement.
